channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Parliament to discuss impeachment of Justice Varma amid political consensus
item.description: The report on Justice Yashwant Varma, an Allahabad High Court judge, is set to be tabled in the Lok Sabha during the upcoming monsoon session starting July 20. Parliamentary Affairs Minister Kiren Rij...

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: England vs Mexico and Brazil's Strategy Against Haaland
item.description: The FIFA World Cup 2026 is intensifying with two pivotal Round of 16 matches on Sunday. England faces Mexico at the iconic Azteca Stadium, marking their return to the venue 40 years after their 1986 W...

item.title: Europe's Debate Over Air Conditioning Amid Record Heatwaves
item.description: Europe is facing a severe heatwave with temperatures reaching unprecedented levels, sparking a cultural and political debate over the use of air conditioning. As record-breaking heatwaves claim thousa...

item.title: Federal Govt Disburses N39.6bn to 24,814 Pensioners, Clears NITEL, PHCN Liabilities
item.description: The Federal Ministry of Finance, led by Minister Taiwo Oyedele, has approved the disbursement of N39.63 billion to 24,814 eligible Defined Benefit Scheme (DBS) pensioners, marking a significant step i...

item.title: England vs Mexico World Cup match details and viewing options
item.description: The article discusses the scheduling challenges for UK fans following the Mexico vs England World Cup last-16 match on 6 July 2026, which starts at 1am BST. It highlights how BBC and ITV will provide ...

item.title: Marilù Tolo: A Legendary Figure of European Cinema in the 1960s and 1970s
item.description: Marilù Tolo, an iconic Italian actress born in 1944 in Rome, rose to prominence during the 1960s and 1970s as a defining figure of European cinema. Her career began as a fashion model before transitio...

item.title: Cancer-Stricken Mother Shares Life Lessons and Resilience in Battling Illness
item.description: Sitawa Kisiangani, a mother from Kitale, has shared profound reflections on how battling cancer has transformed her perspective on life. Despite the challenges, she emphasizes lessons on courage, grat...

item.title: DA proposes 2027 budget funding to boost ube industry and export potential
item.description: The Department of Agriculture (DA) has allocated funding in its proposed 2027 budget to support the country's ube (purple yam) industry, aiming to capitalize on growing global demand. The DA plans to ...

item.title: Appalachia Restaurant in London Offers Authentic Appalachian Cuisine
item.description: Appalachia, a new restaurant near Old Street in London, is making waves with its dedication to authentic Appalachian regional cooking. Chef Ali Borer, formerly of Smoking Goat and Guy Ritchie’s Lore o...

item.title: Federal Government Calls for Justice After Two Nigerians Killed in South Africa
item.description: The Federal Government has strongly condemned the recent killings of two Nigerian nationals in South Africa, urging authorities to investigate and bring perpetrators to justice. The victims, Emeka Cha...

item.title: Canada's Founders' Views on the United States
item.description: The article explores the complex relationship between Canada's founders and the United States, highlighting how early Canadian leaders admired and respected their southern neighbor despite political t...

item.title: Kenyan Teacher's Innovation Turns Banana Waste into Eco-Friendly Textiles in Uganda
item.description: Dr. Kimani Muturi, a former Fine Arts teacher from Kenya, has transformed discarded banana stems into a thriving textile business in Uganda. After moving to Uganda to teach, he noticed farmers discard...

item.title: Momoland Set to Perform in Manila This August
item.description: The K-pop group Momoland, known for their hit song 'Bboom Bboom,' is set to make a triumphant return to Manila this August. The news was announced by ABS-CBN News on July 5, 2026, confirming the group...

item.title: Pakistan Names Babar Azam Captain for West Indies and England Test Series
item.description: The Pakistan Men’s National Selection Committee has officially announced Babar Azam as the captain for the Test series against both West Indies and England. A 16-member squad was selected for the West...

item.title: Bollywood's Portrayal of Kashmiri Resistance and the Controversy Over Pellet Gun Use
item.description: The article discusses the controversy surrounding the Bollywood film 'Chauhaan' and its teaser, which has sparked outrage among Kashmiris. The teaser downplays the severe impact of pellet guns used by...

item.title: Jeffrey Santos shares sibling bond with Judy Ann Santos on Kapamilya Deal Or No Deal
item.description: The article discusses Jeffrey Santos' recent interview where he openly talks about his close relationship with his sister, Judy Ann Santos, during their participation in the Philippine television show...

item.title: Narayan Khandekar's Pigment Collection: A Journey Through Color and History
item.description: Narayan Khandekar, a leading pigment expert at Harvard Art Museum, discusses the Forbes Pigment Collection, a repository of over 3,000 pigments that traces human creativity, trade, and scientific disc...

item.title: Comparing American and European Football Stadiums for the 2026 FIFA World Cup
item.description: A European football fan visiting America for the first time has shared his amazement at the luxurious facilities of a 2026 FIFA World Cup stadium in Dallas, highlighting stark differences between Amer...

item.title: Caleb Amisi's Journey of Loss and Advocacy for National Unity
item.description: Caleb Amisi, a Member of Parliament from Saboti, shared his tragic childhood story of losing both parents before his birth and months after he was born. He emphasized his election by a diverse communi...

item.title: UK Defense Spending Cuts and Nuclear Prioritization Spark Controversy
item.description: The article details criticism of the UK's defense spending plan under Prime Minister Keir Starmer, which allocates significant funds to nuclear programs while reducing budgets for frontline military o...
